lower_acuity_interpretation: Lacerations without bone, tendon, muscle, nerve, or vascular injury :: Full active DIP flexion indicating intact FDP.; Isolated PIP flexion intact, indicating intact FDS.; Full extension.; Normal two-point discrimination on both sides of the injured digit.; Normal capillary refill without vascular compromise.; X-ray shows no fracture or foreign body.
reasoning: This is an isolated finger laceration with no evidence of tendon, nerve, vascular, bone, or foreign body involvement on examination and imaging. Under the ASSH/ACEP guidelines, lacerations without structural injury do not require hand surgery consultation during the initial ED encounter and may not require hand surgery follow-up.

title: Metal Shaving Removed from Hypothenar Eminence
triage_recommendation_text: Does not require hand surgery consultation during the initial emergency department encounter, and may not require hand surgery follow-up.
lower_acuity_interpretation: Uncomplicated foreign bodies that have been removed without deep/structural involvement. :: 3 mm superficial metallic fragment.; Foreign body was located and removed in its entirety.; Full active range of motion before and after removal.; Normal ulnar nerve motor and sensory examination.; No evidence of tendon, nerve, vascular, or deep structural involvement.
reasoning: This is an uncomplicated superficial metallic foreign body that was successfully removed in the ED, with intact motor function, sensation, and range of motion before and after removal. Under ASSH guidance, uncomplicated foreign bodies removed without deep or structural involvement do not require hand surgery consultation during the initial encounter and may not require hand surgery follow-up.

title: Retained Needle in Thenar Eminence
triage_recommendation_text: Does not require immediate hand surgery consultation during the initial emergency department encounter, but most often should receive outpatient hand surgery follow-up.
higher_acuity_interpretation: Foreign bodies unable to be easily removed :: Metallic foreign body is embedded deep within the thenar musculature.; ED exploration under local anesthesia and fluoroscopic guidance failed to locate/remove the foreign body.; Sensation, motor function, and vascular status are intact, with no infection.
reasoning: The key ASSH category is 'foreign bodies unable to be easily removed,' which generally does not require immediate hand surgery consultation during the initial ED encounter but most often warrants outpatient hand surgery follow-up. There are no findings that would escalate to immediate consultation, such as vascular compromise, tendon/nerve injury, infection, open fracture/joint injury, uncontrolled bleeding, or extensive soft tissue trauma.
